Empires & Puzzles Director Zuri 5-Star Legendary Holy Yellow from Starfall Circus Family Review

Director Zuri used to be the gold standard for Holy healers, but has the curtain finally fallen on her performance? We dive deep into her mechanics, minion mastery, and whether her base form can still juggle the demands of the modern Empires & Puzzles meta.
Director Zuri Review: The Original Ringmaster of Restoration
Director Zuri: The Ringmaster Who Refuses to Leave the Stage (But Maybe Should)

Director Zuri, the "Master of Circus," was once the indisputable queen of yellow healers when she debuted in the Starfall Circus event back in 2021. As a 5-star Legendary Holy Sorcerer, she brought a kit that was frankly overloaded for its time: massive healing, beefy minions, and a team-wide cleanse. While her costume has recently given her a new lease on life, this review focuses on her

—the original Director who arguably defined what a "modern" healer should look like before the actual modern era left her stats in the dust. Think of her as the classic act that everyone respects, even if the acrobatics aren't quite as fast as the new performers.

Zuri's design is a fantastic blend of elegance and performance. She sports a top hat that would make any magician jealous, giant angel wings (because why not?), and an outfit that screams "I run this show." The art captures a sense of calm authority—she’s not fighting; she’s directing the chaos. Compared to her clown-themed costume, the base version feels more dignified, like the owner of the circus rather than one of the acts. It's a classic look that has aged better than her defense stat.
Let's address the elephant in the circus tent:

.
With 766 Attack, 772 Defense, and 1421 Health, Director Zuri's base stats are officially a relic of the past.
766 is extremely low. Even 4-star heroes today often surpass this.
772 is paper-thin in a meta where hitters regularly boast 1300+ attack stats. She will melt under tile damage.
1421 is barely a speed bump for modern snipers.

Compared to the current benchmark of ~1300 Defense and ~2400 Health for new 5-stars, base Zuri is incredibly fragile. Without Limit Breaks and max emblems (and realistically, her costume bonus), she is a liability in competitive play.

The Clown Minions (15% HP / 22% Attack) add a layer of "overheal" and physical shielding. In a meta full of DoT (Damage over Time), these meat shields are invaluable.
status ailments is arguably her most important feature. With Defense Down, Mindless Attack, and various DOTs running rampant, a reliable cleanse on an Average speed healer is gold.
The costume bumps the secondary healing to 45% and the minions to 20% HP/30% Attack. While the costume is strictly better, the base skill is functionally very similar—it's the
behind the skill that make the difference.

She essentially resets the board state for your team.
Her low defense means her 1421 HP is worth "less" than a modern hero's HP. She takes significantly more damage from each hit, making her effective survivability much lower than the numbers suggest.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Starfall Circus family bonus is one of the better ones for a healer, providing built-in survivability.
cleanse the hero's most recent ailment

at the end of the turn makes Zuri surprisingly slippery. If she gets hit with a Silence or Mana Block, she has a coin-flip chance (or guaranteed if you run a full family team) to shake it off immediately. This ensures she can actually cast her cleanse when it matters.

reduces damage from Special Skills. This helps patch her low defense against snipers, but it doesn't help against tile damage or slash attacks. It's useful, but not game-changing like "Revive" or "Dodge."

Upon release, Zuri was a Titan. She was the best yellow healer in the game, dethroning Delilah and Vivica. Her ability to practically fully heal a dying tank while cleansing the team made her a staple in War Defense and Offense.

Her skill set has aged gracefully, but her body has not. The 65% heal is still huge, but a 1400 HP hero in 2026 is basically a 3-star. The power creep has moved the baseline so far that base Zuri is now statistically weaker than many modern 4-star costumes.
(mostly). Unless you have her costume to boost her stats, base Zuri is too fragile for Diamond Arena raids or top-tier Wars. However, she remains excellent for
where her mechanics shine and she isn't punished as hard for her low defense.
If you have the costume, you likely won't use this base version much, but here is the breakdown:
The costume version is a massive upgrade. It turns her into a tanky monster with stats that rival modern heroes (1127 Attack / 1384 Defense / 2262 HP).
The costume's skill is slightly better (higher secondary heal, beefier minions), but the real value is the stats.
Even if you prefer the Sorcerer class or the specific art of the base version, the Costume Bonus provides a massive stat stick that makes the base version
more usable, but you are almost always better off using the Costume side.
If you are forced to use the base version (perhaps you need the Sorcerer class for mana delay?), you must go all-in on survivability.
Desperately tries to patch her low defense to keep her alive long enough to fire.
Her minions will be weaker (since minion HP comes from caster HP, but minion attack comes from caster Attack), but a dead healer summons zero minions.
Zuri dies to a stiff breeze.
. Zuri's job is to stay alive and fix the team. She can't do that if she's dead.
The 65% heal to the lowest ally is a lifesaver that few other healers can match.
Removes all ailments, crucial for countering Defense Down and DoT.
Adds an extra layer of HP to the whole team.
High chance to shrug off ailments passively.
Her base survivability is critically low by modern standards.
She offers no offensive pressure (unlike her Costume which reflects ailments).
Heroes like Grimble, Goretooth, or Lunar heroes eat her minions for breakfast.
Still useful in later war flags where you need a reliable cleanser.
Her high healing and minions make auto-farming safe and easy.
The overheal from minions helps survive long boss fights.
Zuri keeps Ludwig alive while he taunts. Ludwig charges the slow hitters. Zuri cleanses the team if Ludwig's taunt wears off or gets bypassed.
Zuri keeps the minion train rolling. Pengi uses the minions to machine-gun the enemy. Note: Vulnerable to minion counters.
destroy her minions or punish her for having them.
prevent her minions from spawning (if treated as a buff/summon) and block the healing if it's regeneration (it's not, it's direct heal, so she is safe there).
Any modern sniper can likely one-shot base Zuri before she fires.

If you pull Zuri early, you have struck gold. Even with "low" stats, she will carry you through Season 2, 3, and 4. Prioritize leveling her as your main healer. She does everything a new player needs: keeps you alive and fixes mistakes (cleanse).

Without the costume, she is a museum piece. If you have the costume, level it immediately. If you only have the base, she is likely a depth hero for War teams 4-6 or Rush Wars where her average speed becomes fast. Do not Limit Break the base version unless you have the costume.

When released, players called for nerfs, screaming that "She heals 65%! That's insane!" and "She makes Delilah look like a 3-star!" It's funny to look back at those comments now in a world of 3000 HP heroes.

Ironically, she actually received buffs post-release (along with other Circus heroes) which confused everyone. "Why buff the strongest healer?" asked the forum users. The answer was likely to push the sales of the portal even harder.
Despite the power creep, she remains beloved for her reliability. One user noted, "She’s the only reason I can finish the Ninja Tower."

Director Zuri (Base) is like a vintage Rolls Royce. She's built with luxury and class, and her mechanics are timelessly excellent. However, her engine (stats) just can't keep up on the modern highway of Empires & Puzzles. She needs her Costume to be competitive, but if you're playing in the lower leagues or PvE, she'll still treat you like royalty.
A mechanical masterpiece trapped in an aging body. Get the costume, and she becomes a goddess again.

Is Director Zuri still the Queen of Healing in 2026? We analyze her stats, minions, and cleanse ability against the modern power creep to see if this Starfall Circus legend still deserves a spot on your team.
